summary: Engine-agnostic C# game-logic core for MontyGame — the platformer-board hybrid for ages 5–7. Pure .NET 8 class library (board, dice, tiles, turns, story beats) + xunit tests + a console playthrough simulator. Unity Personal wraps this later with thin MonoBehaviour shells.

vision.problem: MontyGame's design is locked (Docs/GAME_DESIGN_IDEATION.md) but nothing is playable or testable yet. The game logic — 25-tile World 1 board, dice/card movement, portal/whirlpool/elevator/hyperspace tile effects, forgiving turn loop — needs to exist as verified code before Unity work starts, so Sprint 1 in the editor is wiring, not invention.
vision.success:
dotnet test green with the full rules covered (movement bounds, every tile effect, win condition, turn rotation, seeded determinism)
MontyGame.Cli --auto plays a complete seeded 2-player World 1 game to victory and exits 0
MontyGame.Core has zero UnityEngine references — drop-in ready for the Unity shell
vision.audience: Players: children 5–7 (solo or 2–4 pass-and-play). Developer: Unity shell integrates the core library in Sprint 1.
users: Unity shell developer (consumes the core API)

functional,Characters Dino and Cat,"Create src/MontyGame.Core/Characters.cs: Dino stomp ability (negates one obstacle penalty per world) and Cat pounce ability (+1 movement, once per 3 turns), applied as movement modifiers in the engine",Character abilities per the locked design,should,GAME_DESIGN_IDEATION.md
functional,Story beat events,"Create src/MontyGame.Core/StoryBeats.cs: the engine raises events with World 1 narrative strings at game start, halfway (tile 13), boss tile and victory",Story-driven gameplay is a pillar,should,GAME_DESIGN_IDEATION.md

decisions.title: Engine-agnostic core library
decisions.context: The locked tech stack is Unity, but Unity cannot be exercised by automated validation without a licensed editor in batchmode.
decisions.decision: All game logic lives in MontyGame.Core, a pure .NET 8 class library with zero UnityEngine references; Unity gets thin MonoBehaviour shells that only call the core API.
decisions.rationale: Maximizes the logic that agents and CI can build, test and verify; minimizes untestable editor wiring; standard pattern for testable Unity games.
alternatives: All-in Unity C# scripts (untestable without the editor)
Web prototype (throwaway for the real game)
decisions.consequences: Unity Sprint 1 becomes integration work; the core API must stay engine-neutral (no UnityEngine types in public signatures).

decisions.title: Deterministic seeded RNG
decisions.context: Dice, hyperspace warps and mystery cards are random; fairness and reproducibility matter for a kids' game and for tests.
decisions.decision: Randomness goes through an IRandom interface with a seedable default implementation, injected into the engine.
decisions.rationale: Seeded games are replayable and unit-testable; fairness properties can be asserted.
decisions.consequences: All random behavior must flow through the injected source.

decisions.title: .NET 8 LTS with xunit
decisions.context: The core library needs a long-lived, Unity-compatible C# target and a test framework.
decisions.decision: Target net8.0 (netstandard2.1-compatible API surface where practical) and use xunit for tests.
decisions.rationale: LTS support; xunit is the de-facto .NET test framework; Unity 2022+ consumes modern C# libraries.
decisions.consequences: Avoid APIs newer than what the Unity scripting runtime consumes.

claudeMd:
# MontyGame Core — build context

## Tech Stack
- .NET 8 SDK (LTS), C# 12
- xunit for tests, System.Text.Json for serialization
- No external NuGet packages beyond xunit — the core must stay dependency-free for Unity

## Structure
- `MontyGame.sln` — solution at repo root
- `src/MontyGame.Core/` — pure game-logic class library (Board, Tile, Dice, MovementCards, TileEffects, GameEngine, Characters, StoryBeats, GameState)
- `src/MontyGame.Cli/` — console playthrough simulator (`dotnet run --project src/MontyGame.Cli -- --auto`)
- `tests/MontyGame.Core.Tests/` — xunit suite

## Hard constraints
- **Zero `UnityEngine` references in MontyGame.Core** — it will be consumed by thin MonoBehaviour shells later; keep public API engine-neutral (plain C# types, events/callbacks, no static singletons)
- All randomness through an injected `IRandom` (seedable) — never `new Random()` inline
- Forgiving design: players never drop below tile 1, no eliminations, no instant game-over
- Game rules source of truth: `Docs/GAME_DESIGN_IDEATION.md` and `Docs/WORLD_1_LAYOUT.md`

## Patterns
- Engine exposes state + `TakeTurn()`; UI layers (CLI now, Unity later) render and drive it
- Story beats and tile effects surface as events the shell can subscribe to
- Tests assert rules from the design docs (tile effect ranges, clamping, win at tile 25)
architectureStyle: Architecture: a library/SDK with no application UI.
platforms: Cross-platform .NET 8 (macOS/Windows/Linux) — Unity Personal shell later per the locked stack
teamSkills: Team context: small team or solo — favour simple, low-maintenance stacks; avoid single-person dependency.
testingStrategy: Testing: unit tests for core logic.
deliveryContext: Context: greenfield — a brand-new build from scratch.
monetization: Monetization: one-time purchase or perpetual license.
risksAssumptions: Risk: Unity integration assumptions must hold (core API stays engine-neutral).
